channel.title: DevFocus - 开发者聚焦
channel.description: 每日自动整理的开发者资讯：AI 热榜、GitHub 趋势、技术新闻

item.title: [github_trending] codecrafters-io/build-your-own-x
item.description: Build Your Own X is a collection of step-by-step tutorials that guide you through recreating popular technologies like databases, Git, and Docker from scratch. By building these tools yourself, you gain a deep understanding of how they work under the hood. It's an invaluable resource for developers who want to move beyond using APIs and truly master the fundamentals of programming through hands-on practice.

item.title: [github_trending] microsoft/terminal
item.description: Microsoft has merged the new Windows Terminal with the original console host into one unified project, giving developers access to all command-line tools from a single place. This unified terminal supports tabs, GPU-accelerated rendering, and backward compatibility with the classic console, making it seamless to switch between PowerShell, CMD, WSL, and more. For programmers, sysadmins, and IT pros who live in the command line, it eliminates the hassle of juggling multiple windows and boosts prod

item.title: [github_trending] OpenCut-app/OpenCut
item.description: OpenCut is an open-source video editor designed as a free alternative to CapCut. It offers powerful editing features without any subscription or watermark, making it ideal for content creators who want full control over their tools. The project is cross-platform and community-driven, ensuring transparency and continuous improvement. For anyone tired of paid video editors or restrictive platforms, OpenCut provides a flexible and privacy-respecting solution.

item.title: [github_trending] ocornut/imgui
item.description: Dear ImGui is a bloat-free graphical user interface library for C++ with minimal dependencies, offering a seamless immediate mode GUI experience. It is especially popular among game developers and tool builders who need quick, interactive panels for debugging or real-time data visualization without the overhead of traditional UI frameworks. Its focus on simplicity and performance makes it a go-to choice for indie games and embedded tools.

item.title: [github_trending] PKUFlyingPig/cs-self-learning
item.description: Maintained by a Peking University student known as FlyingPig, this GitHub project offers a clear, structured learning path for self-taught computer science students. It curates free online textbooks, video courses, and exercises covering everything from programming basics to operating systems, networking, and databases, with recommended study order. Career switchers, college students, and hobbyists can use it to build a CS degree-level foundation without getting lost in the sea of information.

item.title: [36kr] DeepSeek V4“满血版”曝光了，最快明天发布
item.description: According to an exclusive report from 36Kr, the full-powered version of DeepSeek V4 is set to drop as soon as tomorrow, marking a major upgrade for this Chinese large language model. The new release is expected to bring substantial improvements in reasoning, multi-turn dialogue, and code generation, potentially rivaling GPT-4-level performance. For domestic AI developers and enterprise users, this signals a technological breakthrough that could lead to more cost-effective and efficient real-worl

item.title: [github_trending] asgeirtj/system_prompts_leaks
item.description: This GitHub repository compiles leaked system prompts from popular AI models like Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Opus 4.8, as well as OpenAI's ChatGPT 5.5 Thinking and GPT 5.5 Instant. By exposing the hidden instructions that shape how these models behave, it offers rare insight into prompt engineering, safety guardrails, and role-setting techniques. AI researchers and developers can learn how top models are orchestrated, but the leak also raises important questions about access control and inte

item.title: [github_trending] Panniantong/Agent-Reach
item.description: Agent-Reach is a CLI tool that gives AI agents the ability to 'see' the entire internet. It enables reading and searching across major platforms like Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, and Xiaohongshu — all without any API fees. The biggest pain point for AI agent developers is getting real-time, cross-platform data; Agent-Reach solves this with a single command. Whether you're building a social media analyzer, a tech trend tracker, or a content aggregator, you can now equip your AI wit

item.title: [github_trending] jamiepine/voicebox
item.description: Voicebox is an open-source AI voice studio that lets you clone voices, dictate text, and create speech content effortlessly. It runs entirely offline, ensuring privacy, and can generate realistic voice output from just a few minutes of audio samples, even making the AI say things it never originally said. Podcasters, voice actors, content creators, and visually impaired users will find it invaluable for production and accessibility, significantly lowering the barrier to professional-grade voice

item.title: [github_trending] usestrix/strix
item.description: Strix is an open-source AI-powered penetration testing tool designed to automatically identify and fix vulnerabilities in applications. By simulating cyber attacks with artificial intelligence, it helps developers catch security flaws early in the development cycle, significantly reducing manual effort and cost. This tool is particularly valuable for startups and individual developers who lack dedicated security teams, offering a practical way to strengthen app security without complex infrastru

item.title: [github_trending] calesthio/OpenMontage
item.description: OpenMontage is the world's first open-source, agentic video production system, featuring 12 pipelines, 52 tools, and over 500 agent skills. It transforms your AI coding assistant into a full-fledged video production studio, automating tasks like scriptwriting, editing, voiceover, and effects. This solves the complexity and high cost of traditional video creation, enabling anyone to produce professional videos using natural language commands. Content creators, marketers, and developers benefit fr

item.title: [36kr] 900 万副 AI 眼镜连夜「上锁」，就因为一颗小白灯
item.description: A massive 9 million AI glasses were remotely locked overnight after a tiny white LED indicator sparked a privacy uproar, as reported by 36Kr. The small light was designed to alert when the camera is recording, but users feared it could be hacked or leak privacy. Manufacturers issued an emergency firmware update that forcibly disabled core functions, rendering countless pairs unusable. This incident highlights the delicate balance between privacy protection and user experience in smart wearables,

item.title: [36kr] 实习生，月入12万
item.description: A recent report about interns earning 120,000 RMB per month has gone viral, sounding like a fantasy but revealing the fierce competition among top tech firms and quant funds for rare talent. These interns, often from elite universities, possess hardcore AI algorithm or financial engineering skills and are locked in with high salaries before graduation. This signals a complete upgrade in talent strategy—young people no longer need to wait for seniority; if their skills are strong enough, they can

item.title: [36kr] 8点1氪丨长鑫科技中签号出炉：共约770.22万个；西班牙1-0战胜阿根廷，夺得本届世界杯冠军；月之暗面有望最快6个月内赴港上市
item.description: Today's 8:01 PM digest brings three major stories: Changxin Memory Technologies (CXMT) released its IPO lottery results with about 7.7 million winning numbers, exciting retail investors; Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 to claim the World Cup title; and AI startup Moonshot AI is reportedly eyeing a Hong Kong listing within six months. These events link three threads—semiconductor localization, global sports, and AI capital markets—showcasing the diverse dynamics of technology and society.

item.title: [36kr] 这，可能是今年WAIC最惊艳的图片！
item.description: At the World AI Conference (WAIC), a stunning AI-generated image went viral—perhaps an artistic digital painting or an eerily realistic virtual character. Dubbed the most breathtaking picture of the event, it pushes the creative boundaries of large models, letting people directly feel AI's explosive power in visual expression. For audiences, it shatters the stereotype that AI can only write text; for the industry, it marks a milestone where multimodal models can produce work rivaling human artis

item.title: [36kr] 预言“互联网泡沫”的峰会，开始讨论“AI泡沫”了
item.description: The annual conference that famously predicted the dot-com bubble has now turned its focus to the potential AI bubble. Attendees are fiercely debating whether today's AI boom mirrors the late 1990s—with overvalued startups, unclear monetization, and a flood of capital. However, others argue that AI today has a much stronger technological foundation and more real-world applications (e.g., healthcare, autonomous driving), so any bubble might be more like localized overheating than a systemic crisis

item.title: [36kr] 端侧大模型成了刚需，芯片准备好了吗？
item.description: As large language models move from cloud to devices like phones, PCs, and cars, on-device inference becomes a necessity—users want low latency and data privacy. But the question is whether current chips can handle the heavy lifting. This article explains that while Qualcomm, Apple, and Huawei are pushing NPUs and memory bandwidth, running multi-billion-parameter models under tight power budgets still faces thermal and compute balance challenges. The good news is that on-device AI is creating a n

item.title: [36kr] 在WAIC，第一次觉得AI不必那么聪明
item.description: Popular on 36Kr: 在WAIC，第一次觉得AI不必那么聪明

item.title: [infoq] WAIC 2026 | 混元Hy3大模型首秀，腾讯上线AI技能支付体系
item.description: At WAIC 2026, Tencent publicly debuted its Hunyuan Hy3 large language model, claiming major leaps in reasoning and multimodal capabilities. Alongside the model launch, Tencent introduced an AI skills payment system that lets users purchase specific AI abilities—like image generation or code assistance—on a per-use or volume basis. This flexible monetization model could reshape how AI services are consumed and billed, moving away from blanket subscriptions toward granular value exchange.

item.title: [infoq] 搭载全球首个智能体原生操作系统 Step AOS，STEPX Neo 亮相 2026 WAIC
item.description: At WAIC 2026, STEPX unveiled the Neo smart device powered by Step AOS, the world's first agent-native operating system. Built specifically for AI agents, Step AOS handles multi-agent collaboration, task scheduling, and resource allocation directly, transforming hardware from a model runner into a host platform for autonomous agent swarms. This gives developers a new infrastructure foundation for building complex agent-based applications that require coordination and real-time adaptation.

item.title: [36kr] Kimi逼得两大巨头改变定价？奥特曼罕见认错，Claude额度重置
item.description: Popular on 36Kr: Kimi逼得两大巨头改变定价？奥特曼罕见认错，Claude额度重置

item.title: [infoq] GOAI 世界人工智能开源大赛官网上线，全球报名正式开启
item.description: The GOAI World AI Open Source Competition has officially launched its website and opened global registration. This international contest focuses on open-source AI technologies, aiming to bring together developers worldwide to tackle real-world AI challenges. It provides massive datasets and computing power, encouraging innovation and fostering the open-source ecosystem. It is ideal for machine learning engineers and researchers who want to make an impact through technology.

item.title: [36kr] 多方猛攻AI入口
item.description: Popular on 36Kr: 多方猛攻AI入口

item.title: [infoq] AI从“能对话”走向“能办事”，蚂蚁集团WAIC展示三层布局
item.description: At WAIC, Ant Group demonstrated how AI is evolving from conversational interactions to executing real tasks, unveiling a three-tier layout covering foundation models, smart applications, and industry solutions. This shift means AI is no longer just a chat tool but can handle complex business operations like payments, risk control, and customer service. It offers efficient, trustworthy intelligent services for financial institutions and SMEs, driving digital transformation across industries.

item.title: [infoq] DA-Nav实现机器人“偏航自纠偏”：找回路线成功率达98%
item.description: Featured on InfoQ: DA-Nav实现机器人“偏航自纠偏”：找回路线成功率达98%

item.title: [infoq] Agent 沙箱体验计划｜点亮Cube Sandbox，在安全沙箱里释放你的Agent能力
item.description: Featured on InfoQ: Agent 沙箱体验计划｜点亮Cube Sandbox，在安全沙箱里释放你的Agent能力

item.title: [infoq] 豆包视频通话背后，火山引擎重构 Agent 时代多模态传输底座
item.description: Volcano Engine has built a new multimodal transmission foundation for the Doubao video call service, reimagining how audio, video, and text data work together in the age of AI agents. This system tackles the latency and compatibility issues of traditional protocols in intelligent interaction scenarios, making real-time understanding and response feel more natural. For enterprises developing multimodal AI applications, it means lower development overhead and a significantly improved user experien

item.title: [infoq] AI 智能体算力消耗过快，传统账单风控跟不上速度
item.description: The rapid consumption of computing power by AI agents is outpacing traditional billing and risk control systems. Current billing architectures fail to capture sudden spikes in resource usage in real time, leaving companies exposed to runaway costs. The article argues for a new generation of risk control that can dynamically detect anomalous consumption patterns and intervene quickly — a critical need for enterprises heavily reliant on large-scale AI services and for cloud providers managing thes

item.title: [infoq] 为什么 AI Agent 拿到数据却不会推理？可观测对象图语义层的设计与开源实践｜AICon深圳
item.description: AI agents can access massive amounts of data but often fail to reason effectively because they lack an observable semantic layer that bridges data and logic. This article introduces the design of an observable object graph semantic layer and demonstrates through open source practice how it enables agents to grasp the meaning behind data and make more accurate decisions. For engineers building AI system architectures and knowledge graphs, this is a key reference for improving agent cognition.

item.title: [infoq] 不止于 4K，火山引擎画质增强让视频从清晰走向细腻
item.description: Volcano Engine's latest quality enhancement technology goes beyond 4K resolution to transform video from merely sharp to truly rich in texture. Using AI algorithms to optimize detail, color accuracy, and dynamic range, it delivers near-real visual experiences even under constrained network conditions. For video platforms, live streaming services, and content creators, this technology significantly improves viewer immersion and retention.

item.title: [hackernews] Kimi K3: Open Frontier Intelligence
item.description: Kimi K3 is an open-source frontier intelligence model designed to democratize state-of-the-art AI capabilities. It tackles the problem of closed-source AI monopolies by allowing developers and researchers to freely experiment, customize, and improve the model. Startups and academic institutions, in particular, can build their own intelligent applications without expensive licensing fees. This open approach is expected to accelerate AI innovation and democratization, making advanced artificial in

item.title: [hackernews] Show HN: I replaced a $120k bowling center system with $1,600 in ESP32s
item.description: A developer replaced a $120,000 bowling center management system with just $1,600 worth of ESP32 microcontrollers. The project demonstrates how low-cost hardware and clever software design can handle core functions like scoring, sensor integration, and user management. For small bowling alley operators or embedded systems enthusiasts, this is an inspiring example of how to achieve professional-grade automation on a shoestring budget.

item.title: [hackernews] AWS: Inaccurate Estimated Billing Data – $1.7 billion
item.description: AWS has admitted to a massive inaccuracy in its estimated billing data, with errors reaching a staggering $1.7 billion. This means many customers may have received wildly incorrect billing estimates, affecting their budget planning and resource allocation decisions. While AWS assures that final charges will be based on precise data, such a large discrepancy undermines trust in cloud cost management tools. For enterprises heavily reliant on AWS, this event highlights the risks of depending solely

item.title: [hackernews] Inkling: Our Open-Weights Model
item.description: Inkling is a newly released open-weights AI model that makes its parameters fully available for download, fine-tuning, and deployment. Unlike many closed-source models that only offer APIs, Inkling breaks the black box: researchers can scrutinize its behavior, and enterprises can customize it on private data without vendor lock-in. This is especially valuable for teams with strict data privacy needs or those requiring highly tailored AI capabilities. By embracing the open-weights philosophy, Ink

item.title: [hackernews] LG monitors silently install software through Windows Update without consent
item.description: Users discovered that LG monitors silently install software through Windows Update without user consent, sparking controversy over privacy and user autonomy. This behavior not only affects system performance but also exposes the risk of hardware vendors using system update channels to force software installations. It is particularly troubling for tech-savvy users who value privacy and a clean system environment.

item.title: [hackernews] The lost joy of music piracy
item.description: A popular Hacker News post reminisces about the joy of music piracy, arguing it wasn't just about getting music for free but about the community-driven discovery and sharing experience. It was filled with the excitement of exploration and a genuine love for music. While streaming offers convenience, it lacks the thrill of digging for rarities and the pride of curating a personal collection. The article prompts readers to reflect on how we consume media in the digital age and what we've lost alon

item.title: [hackernews] Thanks HN for 15 years of support and helping me find my life's work
item.description: A developer posted a heartfelt thank-you to the Hacker News community for 15 years of support, crediting the platform with helping him find his life's work. The brief yet sincere post reflects on how he learned, exchanged ideas, and ultimately defined his career path through HN. It highlights the profound impact a tech community can have on personal growth and resonates with many readers who have experienced the warmth of peer support.

item.title: [hackernews] Microsoft Comic Chat is now open source
item.description: Microsoft has officially open-sourced the code for its classic chat software, Microsoft Comic Chat. Released in the late 1990s, it was renowned for its novel comic-style interface: users selected characters, expressions, and actions to turn conversations into a sequential comic strip, a radical concept at the time. Now open source, developers can explore its inner workings and potentially port it to modern platforms. This is a treasure trove for tech archaeology enthusiasts and developers curiou

item.title: [hackernews] Qwen 3.8
item.description: Qwen 3.8 is the latest open-source large language model from Alibaba's Qwen family, boasting over 38 billion parameters. It delivers significant improvements in mathematical reasoning, code generation, and multilingual comprehension, with faster inference speed and stronger support for both Chinese and English. Because it remains fully open-source, developers, researchers, and enterprises can deploy it locally or fine-tune it for customized tasks. This makes Qwen 3.8 a compelling choice for buil

item.title: [hackernews] Transcribe.cpp
item.description: Transcribe.cpp is a lightweight speech-to-text tool written in C++ that leverages OpenAI's Whisper model with extensive optimizations for CPU-only devices, including Raspberry Pis. It runs entirely offline, starts up quickly, and consumes minimal memory, making it ideal for privacy-conscious users, journalists in remote areas, or developers needing on-device transcription without cloud dependencies. By eliminating the need for a GPU or internet connection, Transcribe.cpp solves the latency and c

item.title: [producthunt] ZooData
item.description: ZooData is a dedicated data layer built specifically for AI agents, addressing the fragmentation and permission headaches that agents face when accessing data from multiple sources. It offers a unified API with real-time data streaming, enabling AI agents to read, write, and sync structured data seamlessly across different systems. Think of it as giving your agent a nervous system for data. For developers and enterprises building complex AI workflows, ZooData cuts down the heavy lifting of data

item.title: [producthunt] Unabyss for Claude
item.description: Unabyss for Claude is a tool that gives Claude persistent memory across different apps and large language models. It retains conversation context even when you switch between software or AI assistants, eliminating the need to repeat yourself. This solves the fragmentation problem for heavy AI users who juggle multiple tools, making workflows smoother and more coherent.

item.title: [producthunt] OpenSEO
item.description: OpenSEO is an open-source SEO toolkit marketed as a free alternative to Ahrefs. It includes website crawling, keyword research, backlink analysis, and rank tracking, all running locally so no subscription or third-party service is needed. This empowers small business owners, independent bloggers, and budget-conscious marketers to access professional-level SEO insights at zero cost, helping them discover content opportunities, monitor competitors, and boost organic traffic. Because the community

item.title: [producthunt] Clark
item.description: Clark is an AI coworker equipped with its own cloud computer, moving beyond simple chatbots to become an agent that can independently operate desktop software, browse the web, and manage files. Running entirely in the cloud, Clark handles complex tasks like compiling reports, sorting emails, or using SaaS tools, then returns the finished work to you. For teams drowning in repetitive office chores, Clark acts as a tireless digital employee that can control existing software without needing API in

item.title: [producthunt] Kimi K3
item.description: Kimi K3 claims to be the world's first open-source model in the 3 trillion parameter class, boasting roughly 3 trillion parameters. This massive scale pushes the frontier of large language model research and democratization, allowing more teams to explore super-large models at lower cost. While training and deployment remain extremely expensive, its open-source nature could spur new applications and academic breakthroughs.

item.title: [producthunt] Pebbles Ai
item.description: Pebbles Ai is an AI sales platform designed for modern B2B teams. It automates routine tasks like lead follow-ups, customer communication, and data analysis by understanding natural language sales processes. This frees up team members to focus on high-value negotiations. For sales teams looking to boost productivity and reduce administrative overhead, it's a practical AI assistant.

item.title: [producthunt] LiveDemo
item.description: LiveDemo is an open-source interactive demo platform designed as a free alternative to paid tools like Storylane, Navattic, and Arcade. It lets you create product tours, simulated walkthroughs, and interactive demos with full code transparency and control. For SaaS companies and product teams, LiveDemo means no vendor lock-in, the ability to tailor demo experiences exactly to their needs, and significant cost savings on licensing. The open-source community can also contribute improvements, makin

item.title: [producthunt] Spycost
item.description: Spycost is a price-tracking and discount verification tool that helps shoppers determine whether a sale is genuine. By simply pasting a product URL, it displays the item's historical price chart, showing the lowest, average, and current prices, and flags inflated original prices and fake discount percentages. For frequent online shoppers who often fall for limited-time deals, Spycost provides transparency before checkout, revealing the true value of a product and preventing impulse buys driven b

item.title: [producthunt] Kobbe
item.description: Kobbe is a privacy-focused web analytics tool that combines traffic measurement with revenue tracking, specifically tailored for e-commerce sites and content creators. Unlike Google Analytics, it does not collect personal data or use cookies, instead relying on anonymized statistics to show key metrics like visitor count, page views, conversion rate, and e-commerce revenue. This makes it an ideal lightweight and compliant solution for small online stores and independent developers who need to un

item.title: [juejin] 小程序上线半年我赚了多少钱？
item.description: An indie developer shared the real revenue and operational data of his expense tracking mini-program called 'Fuzhu' over six months since launch. He iterated from v1.0.0 to v1.6.1, documenting development logs, user growth, and profitability. This article demonstrates the possibility of building a product in spare time, offering a complete postmortem from coding to business. It’s especially useful for programmers exploring side projects.

item.title: [producthunt] Mirage
item.description: Mirage is a tool that transforms your SaaS into a clickable demo in just 90 seconds, with zero coding or design skills required. It solves the problem of time-consuming and technically demanding demo creation, enabling founders, product managers, and sales teams to quickly showcase core features and user experience to potential customers, significantly improving communication efficiency and conversion rates.

item.title: [juejin] Cursor 转 Codex 大半个月，聊聊我的真实感受
item.description: After switching from Cursor to Amazon Codex for over half a month, a developer shared their real experience of the fundamental differences. While Cursor first impressed them with AI-assisted coding, Codex felt like a true super agent that can independently execute tasks, not just an assistant. The author believes Codex represents the next phase of AI programming—evolving from conversational completion to proactive agency. This comparison is invaluable for developers seeking the cutting edge in A

item.title: [juejin] 写给年轻程序员的几点建议-2
item.description: The author offers advice for young programmers in the post-internet-bonus era, emphasizing the need to develop business acumen and writing skills. Given China's huge population, successfully selling a product or writing a viral WeChat article can bring significant wealth. The article argues that market insight and content communication are as crucial as technical skills, making it relevant for early-career programmers looking to diversify their income streams.

item.title: [juejin] 程序员副业 | 2026年4月复盘
item.description: A programmer shares their side project recap for April 2026, responding to readers who urged an update. The author describes an intense period of coding overnight after the new year, finally releasing a new version of their software. This post highlights the significant time commitment and self-discipline required for side projects, along with the fleeting relief of shipping a release. For developers juggling side hustles, this recap resonates deeply—it shows that sustained effort eventually pay

item.title: [juejin] 面试官问你：“AI 能写 80% 的代码了，公司为什么还需要你？”
item.description: The article explores how technical interviews have transformed as AI now writes 80% of the code. Interviewers assume proficiency with AI tools and instead evaluate skills that make developers irreplaceable: problem decomposition, business understanding, architectural thinking, and effective human-AI collaboration. It cautions against over-reliance on AI-generated code and urges developers to cultivate uniquely human competencies for long-term career relevance.

item.title: [juejin] 凌晨睡不着，我给台风巴威写了个追踪网站
item.description: An insomniac developer built a typhoon tracking website for Typhoon Bavi using Claude Fable 5 in the early hours. He aggregated path data from four weather agencies—China's Central Meteorological Observatory, Japan Meteorological Agency, US Joint Typhoon Warning Center, and Taiwan's Central Weather Bureau—onto a single map, making discrepancies instantly visible. This project demonstrates AI-assisted programming's potential and the value of data aggregation in weather education.

item.title: [juejin] 最近公司烧token比招员工还贵，老板干懵了...
item.description: This isn't a joke—it's a real dilemma many companies are facing. After wholeheartedly adopting AI, their token usage costs have actually surpassed the expense of hiring new employees. Uber's CTO revealed that over 90% of their engineers are using AI services heavily. Bosses are staring at the bills in shock: the technology that was supposed to reduce costs and boost efficiency has instead become a new money-burning black hole.

item.title: [juejin] 停更的这一年，All In AI、陪媳妇生娃、还考了个研
item.description: A Juejin author returns after a year-long hiatus, recounting a whirlwind period of diving headfirst into AI, supporting his wife through childbirth, and even squeezing in graduate school exams. Through first-person narration, he shares how he balanced career, family, and personal growth, offering a relatable and inspiring tale for developers navigating life’s major milestones while striving to keep improving.

item.title: [juejin] 2026编程圈很火的10个Skills
item.description: The 10 hottest Skills in programming circles for 2026 refer not to developers' own abilities but to standardized workflow modules for AI coding assistants. These skill packages transform AI from random code generation to structured delivery, covering code review, test generation, documentation, and more. For teams and individual developers exploring AI-assisted programming, mastering these skills is becoming essential for boosting productivity and code quality.

item.title: [juejin] 前后端一起消失：2026 年 7 月，前端正在被彻底“重新定义”
item.description: This provocative article argues that by July 2026, breakthroughs like Google's leaked Gemini 3.5 Pro will erase the traditional boundary between frontend and backend development. AI capable of generating both UI and business logic could make today's specialized roles obsolete, forcing developers to rethink their skill sets. It asks what 'full-stack' means when machines handle the stack, and explores how coders can adapt to a world where the very concept of frontend is being redefined.

item.title: [zhihu] 如何看待月之暗面 kimi 在 2026 年 7 月 19 日宣布算力紧张，暂停会员开放？
item.description: On July 19, 2026, Moonshot AI's Kimi paused new member registrations due to computing power shortage, highlighting the growing compute bottleneck in the AI large model industry. As user demand surges and model training scales up, compute resources have become scarce. This move may drive users to competitors and spark industry-wide debate on AI service sustainability and cost management strategies.

item.title: [zhihu] 全美出现数据中心出售潮，「反数据中心运动」又向红蓝州同时蔓延，这会对市场和 AI 产业形成多大影响？
item.description: A wave of data center sell-offs is sweeping across the United States, while the 'anti-data center movement' spreads in both red and blue states, reflecting rising public concern over energy consumption, land use, and environmental impact. This trend could slow data center construction and increase operational costs, potentially affecting the AI industry's computing supply and deployment pace, especially for cloud-based AI services that rely on massive data center clusters.

item.title: [zhihu] 如何看待 OpenAI 战略未来负责人对 kimi k3 开源的公开发言？
item.description: OpenAI's head of strategic future publicly commented on the open-sourcing of Kimi K3, drawing significant industry attention. As a top AI executive, this remark may signal shifts in OpenAI's stance on open-source strategies or its assessment of the competitive landscape. The comment could fuel further debate on open-source versus closed-source model development paths, as well as the technological rivalry between Chinese AI firms and global leaders.

item.title: 📅 历史归档
item.description: 查看往日精选与趋势变化
